item: Contractor registered in country with a current drilling licence
Contractor has completed at least 5 similar projects in the last 3 years, with referees contacted
Contractor owns or has secured the rig, compressor and support equipment the job needs, and key staff CVs are on file
Contractor financial capacity and litigation history reviewed
Contract uses a bill of quantities and payment is for actual verified quantities, not an estimated lump sum
guidance: Lump sum payment hides real costs; BoQ payment keeps the price honest.

item: Dry hole and low yield payment policy clearly defined, with 'dry' defined by a yield threshold
guidance: Refusing to pay for dry holes pushes the contractor to inflate successful holes or cut corners.

item: Bill of quantities checked for front loading (mobilisation and siting items priced far above their share of the works)
guidance: In a documented fraud case mobilisation and geophysics carried 53 percent of the contract value.

item: Performance bond of 5 to 10 percent of the contract value in place before signature
Independent supervision arranged and paid for, with the supervisor's transport independent of the driller
guidance: A supervisor who depends on the driller for transport or pay is not independent.

item: Drilling rig inspected: mast raised and rig run for an hour without problems (record year of manufacture, manufacturer and lifting capacity)
guidance: Check for oil leaks and have them fixed before giving approval.

item: Compressor run for an hour without problems (record year and manufacturer)
Mud pump and generator ratings checked against the estimated borehole depths and tested
Water tanker checked for leaks
Support trucks available and in working order
Drill pipes adequate to reach the deepest planned borehole
Drill bits and hammer of the correct diameter and right type for the expected ground, in good condition
Geophysical survey equipment working and the personnel competent in its use
Drilling manager and hydrogeologist qualifications and experience recorded and adequate
Rig operator, driver, mechanic and rig assistants experienced (record years of experience)
Record Taker designated by the driller to remain on site at all times and complete all forms
guidance: The Record Taker role should be specified in the contract documents.

item: Site convenient to the point of use with equitable access for all water users, especially women and the most disadvantaged
Land ownership and access rights for users and maintenance teams established and confirmed in writing
Expected yield, renewable resources and water quality sufficient for the intended purpose
Interference with other groundwater sources and natural groundwater flows avoided
Chance of success rated (high, medium or low) and accepted by the client
No potential pollution source within the minimum separation distances (see the separation distance table)
guidance: Pit latrines, burial grounds, refuse dumps, fuel depots, animal pens; where the aquifer is confined the distances can be relaxed.

item: Access allowed and physically possible for the drilling team, equipment and vehicles
Siting report submitted by the driller and approved by the supervisor
Contract signed and the pre-mobilisation meeting held with design, materials and procedures discussed
Programme of works submitted and approved
Community briefed: process explained, roles and contributions agreed, contact persons exchanged, driller's representative introduced
Equipment on site matches what was inspected and is in working condition (drill rods adequate, hammer, bit and temporary casing diameters measured)
Material samples approved against the specification: drilling fluid, casing and screen (length, diameter and wall thickness measured), filter pack and gravel
guidance: Nominal 3 m casing lengths are often actually 2.95 m; measure mid pipe, not at the threaded end.

item: Data collection forms agreed (RWSN Code of Practice Annex E formats)
Project filing system set up: a duplicate hard copy file per site with the checklists printed inside the folder flap
Drill camp layout approved: inflammables away from heat, contaminants away from water and cooking areas, PVC casing and screens shaded from direct sunlight

Health and safety in place: rig away from traffic and power lines, not on a steep slope, public barrier tape up, crew wearing PPE, inflammables stored in marked containers, mast never raised in a thunderstorm, first aid kit and emergency procedure ready
Rig positioned exactly over the pegged site, mast vertical (checked with a spirit level) and the ground stable
guidance: A dry hole must not be attributable to drilling off location.

item: Depth measured with marked drill pipes and logged every metre
guidance: Chalk marks at 1 m intervals numbered from zero; re-mark all pipes if the hammer is changed.

item: Penetration rate recorded every metre
Drilling fluid type recorded and viscosity checked with a Marsh funnel; biodegradable polymer used instead of bentonite where required
Circulation pits hold at least three times the borehole volume and are kept clean (mud drilling)
Cutting samples taken at the agreed interval (1 m default), washed as appropriate, bagged, labelled, photographed and stored in a sample box
guidance: Do not wash soft rocks such as clays; reduce the interval to 0.5 m where the lithology changes rapidly.

item: Strata log prepared from the samples
Water strikes recorded (depth and time) and airlift yield measured with a V plate or a timed bucket
guidance: A handpump needs about 0.3 litres per second.

item: Final drilling depth instructed by the supervisor, not decided by the driller
guidance: For a handpump well with good yield: at least 5 m into the aquifer plus a 3 to 6 m sump below the screen.

item: Daily drilling log signed by the rig operator and the supervisor at the end of every day
Casing and screen assemblage sketched, laid out on the ground, measured piece by piece, totalled against the sketch and photographed before installation
Casing and screens new and to specification, with diameter and wall thickness checked with callipers mid pipe
Joints clean and watertight; solvent cement setting time observed before lowering
Installed casing and screen depth reconciled with the drilled depth; any discrepancy over 3 m resolved by removing, cleaning and reinstalling
Screens placed against the aquifer horizon with a slot size that retains the formation
guidance: Quick check: rub an aquifer sand sample against the screen; fines should pass and coarse grains be retained.

item: Screen open area sufficient for the design yield (open area of at least Q/30 square metres keeps the entrance velocity below 0.03 m/s)
Gravel pack placed as designed and free of mica, clay and laterite
guidance: For fine formations the pack to aquifer D50 ratio should be between 4 and 6.

item: Backfill placed to 6 m below ground and the sanitary seal in the top 6 m (cement slurry at 25 litres of water per 50 kg bag, or bentonite)
Development continued until the water runs clear and sand free, checked by letting samples settle in a clear container
guidance: Some holes clear in hours, some take days; months of pumping to clear indicates the wrong technique was used.

item: Pumping test carried out to specification with rate and water level measured simultaneously against time
guidance: Constant discharge test always; at least a 4 step test for motorised wells; 3 to 6 hours is adequate for a handpump.

item: Sand content at the end of the test no more than 0.2 cubic centimetres in each of three 20 litre samples (10 ppm by volume)
Turbidity within the stipulated limit
guidance: Where excessive turbidity is an aquifer characteristic it is beyond the driller's control.

item: Plumb test passed: the plumb moves freely and deviation does not exceed two thirds of the casing inner diameter per 30 m of depth
Water sampled correctly (1 litre bottle rinsed and labelled; pH, conductivity, iron and bacteriology tested at or near the site) and sent to an approved laboratory
Borehole disinfected with chlorine at 20 mg per litre (1 litre of 0.2 percent solution per 100 litres of well volume) and left for at least 4 hours
Platform cast correctly: shuttering anchored, 1:2:4 mix, 1:50 drainage slope, surface trowelled smooth, borehole number and completion data embedded, kept moist for 72 hours
Final decision recorded: borehole successful or abortive
All installation work complete and approved by the supervisor
All testing complete and the documentation and data handed to the supervisor
Site cleared: waste removed responsibly, circulation pits backfilled and compacted, barrier tape removed, site restored to its former state
Unused materials handled according to the contract and the borehole securely capped
Work completion certificate issued by the supervisor
Drilling report completed with copies to the client, the community and the government regulator
guidance: Data from dry or aborted holes must also be reported.

item: Handover date agreed and the certificate signed by the supervisor, the driller and a community representative
Defect liability monitoring schedule agreed and site visits planned
Problems found during the defect liability period reported to the client and repairs mobilised according to the contract
Supervision invoice submitted and final payment to the driller approved at the end of the defect liability period
Groundwater pH tested before handpump installation; corrosion resistant riser pipes and rods (stainless steel 304/316 or uPVC) used where pH is below 6.5
guidance: Galvanised iron risers can fail within 3 months to 2 years in corrosive water and shed iron and heavy metals into the supply.

item: Sanitary inspection of the completed water point carried out (WHO sanitary inspection format)
Water users have a way to report technical problems during the first one to two years
Third party inspection of the works arranged within 6 months of construction
guidance: Independent inspection with follow up action is the strongest incentive for construction quality.

item: Post construction monitoring visit one to two years after construction, including a repeat water quality test
guidance: A failure within the first one to two years usually points to a design, construction or installation defect.

item: Drilling log, pumping test and water quality records submitted to the authority for successful and unsuccessful boreholes, and the water quality results shared with the community
